What triggered you today? You don't have to fix it — just name it.
What would you tell yourself one year from now?
Describe one moment of peace today, however small.
What are you afraid of right now?
Who helped you stay sober today — even without knowing it?
Write about something you're grieving. Grief is part of healing.
What did your addiction take from you? What are you getting back?
Describe the strongest craving you faced this week. How did you ride it out?
What does freedom feel like to you right now?
Write a letter to the person you were using. You don't have to send it.
What's one thing you did today that the old you wouldn't have done?
Who do you owe an amends to? What would you say?
What's the hardest part of recovery right now? Be honest.
Describe a place where you feel safe. What makes it safe?
What boundary did you set or need to set?
Write about someone who believed in you when you couldn't.
What are three things your body can do today because you're sober?
What does your inner critic say? Now write what your best friend would say instead.
What's one thing you're looking forward to?
Describe a moment this week when you felt genuinely present.
What habit from your using days are you still carrying? What could replace it?
Write about forgiveness — for yourself or someone else.
What does 'one day at a time' mean to you right now?
What would your life look like in five years if you keep going?
What's something you've never told anyone about your addiction?
Write about a relationship that changed because of your recovery.
What's one thing you need but are afraid to ask for?
Describe the difference between loneliness and solitude.
What does surrender mean to you — not weakness, but letting go?
Write about today. Just today. What happened, how you felt, what you noticed.
